name: Radio Garden
description: Spin a 3D globe and tune into live radio stations from every corner of the world. Thrissur, Tokyo, New York — all live.

name: GeoGuessr
description: Dropped into a random Google Street View somewhere on Earth — figure out where you are from visual clues alone.

name: Drive & Listen
description: Virtual drives through major cities around the world with local radio playing. Istanbul at night, NYC in the rain.

name: WindowSwap
description: Look through someone else's window somewhere in the world. New view, new country, every click.

name: Google Arts & Culture
description: Virtual tours of the world's greatest museums and art galleries. The Louvre, MoMA, and 2,000 more — all free.

name: AirPano
description: 360-degree aerial panoramas of the most breathtaking places on Earth. Immersive and genuinely stunning.

name: MapCrunch
description: Click Teleport and get dropped into a random Google Street View location anywhere in the world. A passport-free adventure.

name: Ancient Earth Globe
description: See what Earth looked like 600 million years ago — drag the slider and watch continents collide and split.

name: FlightRadar24
description: Watch thousands of planes moving across the globe in real-time. Click any plane and see its full flight info.

name: NASA Eyes
description: Interactive 3D exploration of the solar system and every NASA mission ever launched. The whole universe in your browser.

name: MarineTraffic
description: Track ships and tankers moving across the oceans in real-time. Every cargo ship, cruise liner, and tanker, live.

name: EarthCam
description: Live webcam feeds from Times Square, Niagara Falls, tropical beaches, and hundreds of places worldwide.

name: Google Earth
description: The classic way to explore our planet — zoom from space to your street. Every mountain, reef, and city.

name: ISS Live Stream
description: Watch the live view from the International Space Station as it orbits Earth every 90 minutes. Real-time space TV.

name: GeoFS
description: Fly a real aircraft anywhere on Earth in your browser using satellite imagery. A surprisingly serious flight simulator.

name: Space Type Generator
description: Type anything and watch each letter come alive with mesmerizing space-themed animations. Oddly satisfying.

name: Weave Silk
description: Draw mesmerizing symmetrical flowing art with your mouse. Every stroke mirrors itself into hypnotic patterns.

name: AutoDraw
description: You doodle roughly; Google's AI instantly suggests what you meant and turns it into clean clipart. Magical.

name: Excalidraw
description: A virtual whiteboard that feels like actual hand-drawn sketches. Perfect for diagrams, brainstorming, or doodling.

name: Photopea
description: A free, browser-based Photoshop clone. Open PSD files, edit photos, design graphics — zero install needed.

name: Canva
description: Easy graphic design for everyone — posters, logos, presentations, social media. Drag-and-drop and done.

name: Pixlr
description: Powerful photo editing right in your browser. Layers, filters, AI tools — no Photoshop subscription needed.

name: Piskel
description: Create animated pixel art sprites directly in the browser. Export for games, GIFs, or just pure fun.

name: Incredibox
description: Drag icons onto beatboxers to create cool layered music loops. Instant music production, zero music knowledge needed.

name: Patatap
description: Turn your keyboard into a musical instrument and visualizer. Every key triggers a unique sound and animation.

name: Chrome Music Lab
description: Google's fun browser experiments with sound, rhythm, and music theory. Learn music through play — actually brilliant.

name: Kleki
description: A simple, distraction-free paint tool in the browser. Clean, fast, and surprisingly capable for digital art.

name: SculptGL
description: Digital 3D sculpting directly in your browser. Shape virtual clay with brushes like a real 3D artist.

name: Behance
description: Browse incredible professional creative portfolios — design, photography, illustration, motion. Endless inspiration.

name: Dribbble
description: Design inspiration from the world's top UI/UX designers. The best place to see what great interfaces look like.

name: FontStruct
description: Build your own fonts from scratch in the browser using a grid-based editor. Design your own handwriting as a typeface.

name: Giphy
description: Search or create your own GIFs. The world's largest GIF library — every emotion, every moment, animated.

name: City Roads
description: Draw all the roads of any city in the world at once — creates stunning map art from real OpenStreetMap data.

name: Zoomquilt 2
description: An infinitely zooming collaborative painting that descends forever through surreal, dreamlike worlds.

name: Paper Me
description: Turn your photos into beautiful paper-cut style art. A uniquely satisfying creative tool in the browser.

name: Noun Project
description: A massive library of clean, minimal icons for everything imaginable. Free to browse and use.

name: Sloyd AI
description: Type any description and get a 3D model generated instantly in your browser. Mind-bending AI creativity.

name: Rodin AI
description: Generate detailed 3D models from text or images for free. Turn a sentence into a fully downloadable 3D asset.

name: NASA APOD
description: A new stunning photo of the universe every single day, explained by a professional astronomer. Always breathtaking.

name: Bartosz Ciechanowski
description: Deeply interactive visual explanations of how everyday things work — GPS, engines, clocks, waves. Pure brilliance.

name: Our World in Data
description: Research and data visualizations showing how the world is changing — poverty, health, energy, happiness. Eye-opening.

name: Scale of the Universe
description: Zoom from the Planck length to the edge of the observable universe. A perspective-shattering journey through scale.

name: 100,000 Stars
description: An interactive 3D visualization of our stellar neighborhood — 100,000 real stars mapped and labeled.

name: Stellarium Web
description: A realistic, real-time sky map showing the exact stars, planets, and constellations above your location right now.

name: HowStuffWorks
description: Clear, well-researched explanations for how everything works — from jet engines to human emotions.

name: Mental Floss
description: Trivia, quizzes, and delightfully weird history facts. The rabbit hole you actually enjoy falling into.

name: Snopes
description: The internet's original fact-checker — busting myths, viral rumors, and misinformation since 1994.

name: Zooniverse
description: Participate in real scientific research as a citizen scientist — classify galaxies, transcribe old manuscripts, find penguins.

name: Worldometer
description: Real-time statistics on global population, deaths, births, energy use, and more — all ticking live.

name: Space.com
description: The best source for space news, cosmic photos, and explanations of everything happening beyond Earth.

name: Atlas Obscura
description: A guide to 25,000+ hidden wonders, curious places, and unusual landmarks around the world. The weird travel bible.

name: Evolution of Trust
description: An interactive game theory guide on why cooperation and trust evolve. Surprisingly moving and thought-provoking.

name: Wait But Why
description: Long, deep, stick-figure illustrated articles on life, AI, Elon Musk, consciousness, and the universe. Brilliantly written.

name: Information is Beautiful
description: Complex data turned into stunning, insightful visual charts. Facts and statistics made genuinely beautiful.

name: Internet Live Stats
description: Watch the internet grow in real-time — emails sent, tweets posted, Google searches, websites created, all live.

name: MapFight
description: Compare the real size of any two countries side by side. Shockingly reveals how distorted most world maps are.

name: True Size of Countries
description: Drag any country across the Mercator map to see its actual geographic size. Greenland is not bigger than Africa.

name: World Monitor
description: A live dashboard of the world — population, births, deaths, CO2, internet usage, all ticking in real time.

name: Wolfram Alpha
description: Type any question — math, science, history, nutrition — and get precise computed answers. The world's smartest calculator.

name: Capitol Trades
description: Track real stock trades made by US politicians. See exactly what senators are buying and selling in real-time.

name: Wikimedia Commons
description: Millions of freely usable images, audio, and video from around the world. An endless visual archive of everything.

name: Finviz
description: A real-time financial market heatmap showing the entire stock market as a color-coded visual grid. Instantly readable.

name: Neal.fun
description: A goldmine of web toys — spend a billion dollars, dive to the bottom of the sea, watch the universe grow.

name: The Useless Web
description: Click a button and get sent to a random, hilariously pointless website. Pure, unfiltered internet chaos.

name: Pointer Pointer
description: Move your mouse anywhere on the page and it finds a photo of someone literally pointing at your cursor.

name: Find the Invisible Cow
description: Move your mouse to find an invisible cow guided only by an increasingly loud MOO. Peak internet absurdity.

name: Hacker Typer
description: Open it, start typing, and look like a Hollywood hacker. Any key you press generates lines of code.

name: Floor796
description: An endlessly expanding animated pixel-art scene packed with hundreds of pop culture easter eggs to find.

name: EmuPedia
description: Play thousands of retro games — DOS, SNES, Game Boy, Arcade — directly in your browser. Pure nostalgia.

name: Contexto
description: Guess the secret word — every guess is ranked by AI semantic similarity. Like Wordle but for big brains.

name: Letroso
description: A daily word puzzle where you unscramble letters to find hidden words. Fast, fun, and surprisingly addictive.

name: Conexo
description: Group 16 words into 4 hidden categories. A daily puzzle that tests lateral thinking and vocabulary.

name: Little Alchemy 2
description: Combine elements (Earth + Fire = Lava) to create new items. Over 700 combinations to discover.

name: Quick, Draw!
description: Can Google's neural network guess what you're drawing in 20 seconds? A globally addictive drawing game.

name: Akinator
description: The web genie that guesses the character you're thinking of in just 20 questions. Eerily accurate.

name: Sporcle
description: Thousands of trivia quizzes on every topic imaginable — geography, music, movies, sports, science.
The classic addictive sliding tile math game. Combine same-numbered tiles to reach 2048. Deceptively simple.

name: Cookie Clicker
description: The original idle game — click a cookie and build a cookie empire. Warning: ruins productivity completely.

name: GeekTyper
description: Mash any key and watch Hollywood-style hacker code fly across the screen. Great for pranking non-tech friends.

name: Slither.io
description: Multiplayer snake game in your browser — grow longer by eating glowing dots, avoid other snakes. Wildly addictive.

name: Paper.io
description: Conquer territory by drawing squares on the map. But don't let other players cut your trail — they'll eliminate you.

name: Agar.io
description: Eat smaller cells to grow larger — multiplayer browser game, zero setup. The original viral .io game.

name: Chess.com
description: Play chess against friends, bots, or strangers. The world's largest chess platform with puzzles and lessons.

name: Lichess
description: Free, open-source chess with no ads, no paywalls, no nonsense. Just chess, beautifully made.

name: Tetris
description: The official free online version of the world's most famous puzzle game. Stack blocks. Clear lines. Don't stop.

name: Jigsaw Explorer
description: Online jigsaw puzzles from any image — hundreds of pieces, relaxing, and surprisingly satisfying to complete.

name: Crossword Labs
description: Play or build your own crossword puzzles online. Clean interface, great for teachers and trivia lovers.

name: Sudoku.com
description: Classic number logic puzzles from easy to expert. Clean design, no ads interrupting your focus.

name: Typeracer
description: Race other people by typing quotes as fast and accurately as possible. The most fun way to improve WPM.

name: Git City
description: Visualize any GitHub profile as a 3D pixel-art city — buildings represent repos, activity lights up the skyline.

name: Artikel Game
description: A fast game to learn German articles (der, die, das) through quick-fire rounds. Great for language learners.

name: Zoom Quilt
description: An infinitely zooming piece of collaborative art — different artists, one endless visual journey. Deeply trippy.

name: Radiooooo
description: A musical time machine — pick any decade and country and listen to what was actually playing on the radio.

name: My 90s TV
description: A simulated 90s TV experience with real commercials, shows, and music videos playing on loop. Pure nostalgia.

name: TV Garden
description: Watch free live TV channels from countries all over the world — news, entertainment, and sports all in one place.

name: A Soft Murmur
description: Mix ambient sounds — rain, thunder, waves, coffee shop — to custom-blend your perfect focus or sleep background.

name: MyNoise
description: High-quality, customizable audio soundscapes for focus, sleep, or relaxation. The best ambient sound generator.

name: Rainy Mood
description: Infinite, looping rain sounds. Just open it and feel instantly calmer. The simplest relaxation tool on the internet.

name: The Oatmeal
description: Hilarious, insightful comics and quizzes about cats, grammar, mantis shrimp, and the absurdity of life.

name: Project Gutenberg
description: 60,000+ free eBooks — every classic ever written, from Dickens to Dostoevsky. Completely free, forever.

name: Medium
description: Long-form articles on every topic by writers who actually know what they're talking about. Many stories are free.

name: Goodreads
description: Find your next favorite book based on what you've loved before. Track your reading and see friend recommendations.

name: The Marginalian
description: Maria Popova's stunning writings on art, science, history, literature, and what it means to live a good life.

name: Aeon
description: Provocative, beautifully written essays on philosophy, science, culture, and the deep questions of existence.

name: Fable
description: A social app for bookworms and binge-watchers — discover what your friends are reading and watching.

name: Story Tribe
description: A collaborative storytelling app where you and friends co-write stories together. Creativity meets social.

name: Auto Catalog Archive
description: Browse thousands of original car brochures and catalogs from the 1950s to today. A paradise for car lovers.

name: Brilliant
description: Learn math, logic, science and CS through bite-sized interactive challenges. The smartest learning app online.

name: Typing Club
description: Learn to touch-type fast with gamified, structured lessons. You'll thank yourself for every year after.

name: Khan Academy
description: Free world-class education in math, science, computing, and more. Actually good for all ages, genuinely.

name: Regexr
description: Learn and test Regular Expressions in real-time with visual highlighting and a cheat sheet. A developer must-have.

name: Learning Music by Ableton
description: Learn music fundamentals through interactive, playable browser lessons — beats, chords, scales, and basslines.

name: 21st.dev
description: Discover and copy community-made UI components instantly. The GitHub for beautiful interface building blocks.

name: Entertrained — Typing
description: Improve your typing speed by typing actual famous books. Get faster at typing while finishing a novel.

name: Mind Luster
description: Over 300,000 free online courses from top providers — all in one searchable place. Learn anything, anytime.

name: Coursiv
description: Learn AI skills for work, business, and side hustles through short, structured courses. Practical, not theoretical.

name: HDLBits
description: Practice digital logic and Verilog HDL through hundreds of progressive circuit design exercises with instant feedback.

name: Runable
description: Run, test, and share code snippets interactively in the browser. Clean interface, instant results, no setup needed.

name: Exercism
description: Practice coding in 67+ languages with mentor-reviewed exercises. The best free platform for getting genuinely good.

name: Coddy
description: Bite-sized interactive coding challenges that teach you to code through doing. Clean UX, great for beginners.

name: Alison
description: Over 5,000 free online courses with real certificates — IT, languages, business, and more. Actually free.

name: Diode — Circuit Simulator
description: Build and simulate electronic circuits in 3D in your browser. Drag components, connect wires, watch it work.

name: AFFiNE
description: An open-source all-in-one workspace — notes, docs, and whiteboards combined. The privacy-first Notion alternative.

name: Pomoflight
description: A beautiful Pomodoro timer that turns your focus sessions into a flight journey. Work feels like travel.

name: Expresso
description: Paste any text and instantly see overused words, sentence length stats, and writing style issues. A writer's mirror.

name: Remove Paywall
description: Paste any paywalled article URL and read it for free. Access NYT, Bloomberg, and more without a subscription.

name: Airmore AI
description: A collection of free AI tools — image generation, writing, voice, video, and more. All in one, no login needed.

name: Muscle Wiki
description: Click any muscle on a 3D body map and instantly see the best exercises for it. The best free fitness reference.

name: JMail
description: Instant disposable email addresses — no signup, no password. Perfect for avoiding spam when trying new services.

name: White Screen
description: A pure white screen for your monitor — useful for screen cleaning, photography lighting, or blanking distractions.

name: Archive.org
description: The Wayback Machine — see what any website looked like in the past. Plus millions of free books, films, and music.

name: CamelCamelCamel
description: Track Amazon price history for any product. Never pay full price again — see the lowest price it's ever been.

name: ManualsLib
description: Find the user manual for any device you've ever owned. Millions of manuals, completely free to download.

name: 10 Minute Mail
description: A temporary email that auto-deletes after 10 minutes. Use it when a site demands your email but you don't trust it.

name: Eat This Much
description: Automated meal planner based on your calories and dietary preferences. Plan your whole week of eating in minutes.

name: JustWatch
description: Find which streaming service has the movie or show you want to watch. Netflix, Prime, Hotstar — all at once.

name: Privnote
description: Send notes that self-destruct after being read once. Share passwords or secrets safely, then they're gone.

name: Ninite
description: Install or update all your essential software at once with one click. No toolbars, no bloatware, no fuss.

name: Dictation.io
description: Type with your voice, right in the browser — free, accurate, works in 60+ languages. No install required.

name: Finviz Markets
description: The full stock market as a live heatmap — see which sectors are up and down at a glance, every trading day.

name: The Telephoner
description: Get randomly connected to a stranger anywhere in the world via a phone call simulation. Weird, wholesome, and oddly addictive.

name: Slow Roads
description: An endlessly generated, peaceful driving game through stunning landscapes. Set it to autopilot and just watch the world go by.
